home.tagline: Every public contract can be audited. We do it, every day, for 50 cities.
home.subtitle: The Fiscal Agents read official gazettes and identify irregularities based on explicit legal criteria.
home.hero_stat_desc: Daily pipeline · alerts with legal basis · source always cited
home.step_collect_desc: Daily monitoring of Brazilian municipal official gazettes via Querido Diário (OKFN Brazil).
home.step_analyze_desc: Fiscal agents cross-reference company IDs (CNPJs), procurement records, federal sanctions, and contract history.
home.step_alert_desc: Automatic publication of findings with risk score ≥ 60 and confidence ≥ 0.70, always with legal basis cited.
home.section_ecosystem: Standing on the shoulders of giants
home.ecosystem_desc: Fiscal Digital extends — not competes with — the foundational Brazilian civic innovation projects.

ecosystem.section_title: Standing on the shoulders of giants
ecosystem.section_desc: Fiscal Digital extends — not competes with — the foundational Brazilian civic innovation projects. Every finding we publish links back to the original data on Querido Diário. We never replicate.
ecosystem.serenata_desc: A pioneer in applying AI to oversight of Brazilian public spending. Has monitored federal congressional expense reimbursements (CEAP) since 2016. Our Fiscal Agents approach is directly inspired by Rosie.
ecosystem.qd_desc: The infrastructure that has digitized the official gazettes of hundreds of Brazilian municipalities. It is our primary data source. Without Querido Diário, Fiscal Digital would not exist.
ecosystem.position_body: was built on top of these two projects. We extend the Brazilian civic innovation ecosystem to the municipal level — using Querido Diário's data with the Serenata method.

home.support_desc: Fiscal Digital is maintained by community contributions. Every inflow and outflow is publicly reported.
home.problem_desc: Brazilian municipal gazettes publish, every day, spending decisions that almost no citizen has time to read. Waivers, contract amendments, appointments and procurement slip by unnoticed for lack of time, tools or technical fluency. Fiscal Digital reads these documents for you and surfaces what deserves attention — always with a link back to the original record.

stats.error_desc: Live counters will return shortly. In the meantime, territorial coverage is confirmed:

home.fiscais_desc: Ten autonomous agents watch over different facets of municipal public spending.

licitacoes.title: Procurement Auditor
licitacoes.detects: Spots unjustified waivers, contract splitting and suspicious non-bidding awards.
licitacoes.law: Law 14.133/2021 — Art. 75 (BRL 100k works / BRL 50k services cap)

contratos.title: Contract Auditor
contratos.detects: Flags amendments above 25% of the original value and extensions beyond the legal limit.

fornecedores.title: Supplier Auditor
fornecedores.detects: Surfaces CNPJs less than 6 months old at contract date and concentration above 40% per department.

pessoal.title: Personnel Auditor
pessoal.detects: Detects appointment spikes in election periods and abnormal turnover in commissioned roles.

home.featured_desc: The latest finding published by the pipeline.

featured.empty_desc: The pipeline is backfilling the Caxias do Sul municipal administration (2021–present). The first public findings will appear here.

how.title: How it works
how.subtitle: From gazette publication to alert — in under 24 hours
how.step_collect_what: Querido Diário retrieves the official municipal gazettes published that day.
how.step_collect_why: Official gazettes publish municipal acts every business day — that is where public spending becomes law. No one reads it all.
how.step_extract_what: An AI model identifies company IDs (CNPJ), amounts, contract names, and the department involved.
how.step_extract_why: Turning a PDF into structured data is what makes cross-referencing at scale possible.
how.step_analyze_what: 10 Fiscal Agents apply legal rules — for example, an amendment above 25% violates Art. 125 of Law 14.133.
how.step_analyze_why: Each Agent watches a specific legal boundary, with documented criteria and explicit legal basis.
how.step_alert_what: Findings with risk ≥ 60 and confidence ≥ 0.70 become public cards linked to the original gazette.
how.step_alert_why: The thresholds ensure only findings with solid evidence are published — minimizing false positives.

home.cities_desc: Cities already registered in Fiscal Digital, grouped by region.

cities.summary: {active} active cities out of {total} registered — grouped by region.
cities.top_subtitle: Where we're spotting most signals — ranked by published findings

open_source.desc: The entire engine, infrastructure and website are open source. Inspect, audit, contribute — every piece is in a public repository.

home.section_contribute: Want to contribute to the project?
home.contribute_desc: Every contribution helps — code, data, ideas, or financial support. Here is how to start.

contribute.section_desc: Every contribution strengthens public oversight. Choose how you want to participate.
contribute.code_desc: Open issues, review PRs, or implement a new Fiscal Agent. The repository is public and every relevant technical decision is discussed openly.
contribute.data_desc: Suggest a new city, validate a finding, or report a false positive. Your human review calibrates the system.
contribute.spread_desc: Share alerts, write about the project, or use the data in news stories. CC-BY 4.0 — always with attribution.
contribute.financial_desc: Recurring support on Catarse or via GitHub Sponsors. Every inflow is public. Catarse coming soon.

manifesto.title: Manifesto
manifesto.subtitle: The principles guiding every line of code and every alert we publish.
manifesto.mission_body: Fiscal Digital is an autonomous oversight agent for Brazilian municipal public spending. It turns official gazettes into verifiable alerts for the public. Initial coverage: Caxias do Sul (RS) — the Adiló Didomenico administration (2021–present).
manifesto.principle_source_body: Every finding links to the original official gazette via Querido Diário. No clickable URL, no publication. This is a project principle, not editorial preference.
manifesto.principle_factual_body: Factual language — “we identified”, “the document indicates”. Never accusatory. We hold no judicial authority to assert criminal conduct; we report what the document shows and let the public decide.
manifesto.principle_transparent_body: Each alert explains why it was generated: which Fiscal Agent triggered it, which rule applied, which law forms the legal basis. The code is public at github.com/fiscal-digital.
manifesto.principle_verifiable_body: Anyone can open the gazette URL and check the evidence. Nothing is a black box. The raw data stays on Querido Diário's infrastructure — we never replicate it.
manifesto.principle_retraction_body: A published mistake becomes a correction on the same channel and with the same reach as the original. This is policy, not courtesy. Acknowledge, repair, update the process.
manifesto.ecosystem_intro: Fiscal Digital stands on two foundational projects of Brazilian civic innovation. We do not compete — we extend.
manifesto.ecosystem_serenata_body: A pioneer in applying AI to oversight of Brazilian public spending. Has monitored federal congressional expense reimbursements (CEAP) since 2016. Our Fiscal Agents approach is directly inspired by Rosie.
manifesto.ecosystem_querido_body: The infrastructure that has digitized the official gazettes of hundreds of Brazilian municipalities. It is our primary data source. Without Querido Diário, Fiscal Digital would not exist.
manifesto.ecosystem_fiscal_scope: Municipal · intelligence layer over QD data
manifesto.ecosystem_fiscal_body: An intelligence and alerting layer on top of the data Querido Diário makes available. Every finding links back to the source on QD. We never replicate the data.
manifesto.ecosystem_position: Position: we extend, we do not compete.
manifesto.ecosystem_position_body: Fiscal Digital exists because Serenata and Querido Diário exist. Every public piece of ours credits both. It is narrative truth and strategy: we anchor legitimacy in the network that already exists.

sobre.title: About
sobre.subtitle: Who we are, where we sit in the ecosystem, and how we operate inside.
sobre.origin_p1: Brazil has more than 5,500 municipalities. Each one publishes an official gazette with contracts, procurement, appointments, and spending. That is millions of pages per year — public data, accessible by law, but impossible to follow at a human scale.
sobre.origin_p2: In 2016, the Serenata de Amor project demonstrated that algorithms can identify anomalous patterns in public spending. In 2019, Querido Diário digitized the official gazettes of hundreds of Brazilian municipalities. Fiscal Digital connects these two legacies: it uses the Querido Diário archive as its source and the Serenata principle as its method.
sobre.who_body_1: Fiscal Digital is a non-partisan, non-governmental civic initiative. We operate as an autonomous agent: five specialized Fiscal Agents read the official gazettes published by Querido Diário (OKFN Brazil) every day, cross-reference public data from the Brazilian Federal Revenue Service and the CGU (Brazil's Federal Office of the Comptroller General), and publish verifiable alerts when they identify patterns that warrant public review.
sobre.who_body_2: We are not journalists, not auditors, not a control authority. We are a digital agent that reads public documents methodically and cites the source for everything it publishes.
sobre.position_body: There is a clear division of roles in Brazilian civic oversight. Fiscal Digital occupies the intelligence layer over municipal data — the missing layer between Querido Diário's open-data infrastructure and an organized reading by specialized agents.
sobre.governance_body: All code is public on GitHub. Changes go through open discussion before merge. No relevant technical decision happens outside the public repository.
sobre.governance_flow: issue → public discussion → fork → PR → review → merge
sobre.repos_engine_role: Engine (Fiscal Agents + Skills + API) and Terraform
sobre.licenses_body: We follow the Querido Diário (OKFN Brazil) standard. Code under a permissive license, data and alerts under a license that requires attribution.
sobre.licenses_mit: All code in the main repositories. You may use, modify, and redistribute it, including commercially, as long as the copyright notice is preserved.
sobre.licenses_ccby: All published alerts, derived datasets, and analytical materials. You may reuse them freely, provided Fiscal Digital is credited as the source.

transparencia.title: Transparency
transparencia.subtitle: How we decide to publish, where the data comes from, how you contribute, and how we correct ourselves when we are wrong.
transparencia.criteria_body: A finding only becomes a public alert when it crosses two thresholds at once. Findings below either threshold are kept internally for review but not published.
transparencia.criteria_risk_desc: Composite risk score computed by the Chief Fiscal from the collected evidence. Considers value, recurrence, and applicable legal basis.
transparencia.criteria_confidence_desc: Degree of certainty in the extraction from the official gazette. Below this threshold there is textual ambiguity that warrants human review.
transparencia.sources_body: Every source used by Fiscal Digital is public and free of charge. We do not buy data, we do not receive leaks, we do not scrape private portals.
transparencia.source_querido_body: Already-indexed municipal official gazettes. Public API access. The backbone of Fiscal Digital: without Querido Diário, the project would not exist.
transparencia.source_receita_body: Registration status, partners, and incorporation date of companies. Public and free access. Used to identify newly opened suppliers and concentration patterns.
transparencia.source_cgu_body: National registries of debarred and suspended companies (CEIS) and of punished companies (CNEP). Available as CSV on the dados.gov.br portal. Used to flag suppliers under active sanctions.
transparencia.source_tse_body: Cross-referencing campaign donors with subsequent suppliers of the municipality. Under study.
transparencia.source_tce_body: Findings and audits from the State Audit Court of Rio Grande do Sul. Under study.
transparencia.source_portal_body: Federal transfers to the municipality, to contextualize the available budget. Under study.
transparencia.retraction_body_1: Being wrong is part of the method. What cannot happen is the error remaining without a public correction. When we identify that an alert was published with a misreading of the document, we open a public issue tagged false-positive in the main repository.
transparencia.retraction_body_2: The correction is published on the same channel and with the same reach as the original. We acknowledge the error clearly, remove the original alert, and update the review process to prevent recurrence.
transparencia.retraction_quote: Same channel, same reach, same honesty. Trust is rebuilt by repairing, not by minimizing.
transparencia.contrib_body: Every relevant technical decision goes through public discussion before merge. There is no private decision channel. The flow is simple and linear:
transparencia.contrib_pr_title: A PR that changes Fiscal Agent logic must include three things
transparencia.contrib_pr_req_1: Legal reference — which law and which article underpins the criterion applied.
transparencia.contrib_pr_req_2: An example of a real official gazette that triggers the alert.
transparencia.contrib_pr_req_3: An example that must NOT trigger the alert — to prevent false positives.
transparencia.costs_link_title: What it costs to run us
transparencia.costs_link_desc: AWS spend collected by FiscalCustos directly from Cost Explorer, with PTAX BCB conversion.

transparencia_custos.title: Operating costs
transparencia_custos.subtitle: Every outflow in public, refreshed daily. The same verifiability standard we apply to municipal spending we apply to ourselves.
transparencia_custos.mtd_desc: Spend so far this month (USD converted at the Brazilian Central Bank PTAX rate).
transparencia_custos.projected_desc: Linear projection at current pace: mtd ÷ days elapsed × days in month.
transparencia_custos.delta_desc: Difference between current mtd and the closed total of the previous month.
transparencia_custos.breakdown_body: Cost distribution for the current month across the AWS services that run the pipeline. Bedrock (Nova Lite + Haiku 4.5) usually dominates; Lambda, DynamoDB and CloudFront are a low and stable baseline.
transparencia_custos.trend_body: Daily cost in BRL over the last {days} days. Spikes on backfill days (historical processing for new cities) are expected.
transparencia_custos.method_body_1: A scheduled Lambda (FiscalCustos) runs daily at 03:00 BRT, queries AWS Cost Explorer with daily granularity grouped by service, persists to a dedicated DynamoDB table, and the site reads from there.
transparencia_custos.method_body_2: USD→BRL conversion uses the Brazilian Central Bank PTAX selling rate (SGS series 1) for the day. The factor is recorded together with the snapshot — auditable after the fact.
transparencia_custos.method_body_3: FiscalCustos itself costs about USD 0.30/month to run (Cost Explorer charges USD 0.01 per call).
transparencia_custos.empty_state: FiscalCustos collecting the first snapshot — back online within 24h.

apoie.title: Support Fiscal Digital
apoie.subtitle: Maintained by community contribution. Every inflow and outflow is public.
apoie.why_body_1: Fiscal Digital is sustained by community contribution — no government sponsorship, no party sponsorship, no reliance on a single funder. That design is what protects the project's editorial independence.
apoie.why_body_2: Your contribution covers the language model that extracts entities from gazettes, the AWS serverless infrastructure, and the domain registration. Everything is accounted for in public.
apoie.catarse_body: The primary support channel is Catarse Recorrente — the same platform used by Querido Diário. Small monthly contributions keep the project's continuous operation alive.
apoie.catarse_model: Why Catarse: it is the most established Brazilian crowdfunding platform for civic projects, with a transparent fee and accountability built into the tool itself.
apoie.github_body: For those who prefer to support code development directly, the alternative channel is GitHub Sponsors. Especially suited for companies and developers who already consume the open-source ecosystem.
apoie.finance_body_1: Every inflow is public on the Catarse page. Every outflow — language-model cost, AWS, domain — is reported periodically in an open report on the fiscal-digital-analytics repository, with auditable CSV files and charts.
apoie.finance_body_2: There is no opaque balance. There is no uncategorized expense. The same standard of verifiability we apply to municipal public spending we apply to ourselves.
apoie.cost_llm_desc: Entity extraction from gazettes and generation of alert narratives with prompt caching.
apoie.cost_aws_desc: Lambda, DynamoDB, SQS, API Gateway. Low-cost architecture sized to actual usage.
apoie.cost_domain_desc: Domain registration, Route 53, CloudFront, and public certificates.
apoie.finance_quote: We hold ourselves to the same standard of verifiability we apply to municipal public spending.

fiscais.title: Autonomous Fiscal Agents
fiscais.subtitle: Fiscal Digital operates with 10 specialized agents, each with explicit criteria, documented legal basis, and calibrated confidence thresholds. Full criteria are available in the source code at github.com/fiscal-digital.

geral.name: Chief Fiscal
geral.role: Orchestrates the 9 specialized Fiscal Agents and identifies recurring patterns across entities.
geral.description: The Chief Fiscal consolidates findings from the 9 specialized Fiscal Agents. When the same supplier accumulates findings across multiple Agents, the base risk score applied is 90, plus 2 points per additional finding.
geral.legal: Has no independent legal basis. Applies the consolidated criteria of the specialized Fiscal Agents.

licitacoes.name: Procurement Fiscal Agent
licitacoes.role: Detects irregular no-bid waivers and contract splitting.
licitacoes.criteria_1: Procurement via no-bid waiver above the threshold for civil works and engineering services — R$ 130,984.20 (Law 14.133/2021, Art. 75, Inc. I; Decree 12.807/2025, effective 01/01/2026).
licitacoes.criteria_2: Procurement via no-bid waiver above the threshold for other services and goods — R$ 65,492.11 (Art. 75, Inc. II; same decree).
licitacoes.criteria_3: Multiple waivers to the same supplier within a short period, with values individually below the threshold — indication of contract splitting prohibited under Art. 75, §1º.
licitacoes.exclusion_1: Waiver grounded in urgency (Art. 75, Inc. VIII) with documented justification in the published act.
licitacoes.exclusion_2: Values below the legal thresholds with no history of splitting to the same supplier.
licitacoes.legal: Law 14.133/2021, Art. 75, Inc. I and II; §1º (prohibition of contract splitting). Values updated by Decree 12.807/2025.

contratos.name: Contracts Fiscal Agent
contratos.role: Detects contract amendments above the legal caps and contracts with excessive duration.
contratos.criteria_1: Contract amendment with an addition exceeding 25% of the original contract value, for civil works, services and goods in general (Law 14.133/2021, Art. 125, §1º, Inc. I).
contratos.criteria_2: Contract amendment with an addition exceeding 50% of the original value in building renovation or equipment contracts (Art. 125, §1º, Inc. II).
contratos.criteria_3: Continuous-service contract with total accumulated duration exceeding 10 years (Art. 107, caput).
contratos.exclusion_1: Qualitative amendments (technical specification changes without value addition) do not constitute this type.
contratos.exclusion_2: Extensions within the legal limits and with documented public-interest justification.
contratos.legal: Law 14.133/2021, Art. 125, §1º, Inc. I and II; Art. 107, caput.

fornecedores.name: Suppliers Fiscal Agent
fornecedores.role: Detects contracts with newly incorporated companies and abnormal supplier concentration.
fornecedores.criteria_1: Contract with a legal entity whose incorporation date at the Federal Revenue Service is less than 180 days before the contract signing date — indicative of a company with no operating history.
fornecedores.criteria_2: Supplier representing more than 40% of the total contracted value by a municipal department over the past 12 months, suggesting abnormal concentration.
fornecedores.exclusion_1: New CNPJ in small-value contracts (below the Art. 75 thresholds).
fornecedores.exclusion_2: Concentration in a single supplier when the nature of the object justifies exclusivity (documented sole-source justification).
fornecedores.legal: Federal Constitution, Art. 37, caput (equal treatment); Law 14.133/2021, Art. 9º (principle of competitiveness). Company data via Federal Revenue API (BrasilAPI).

pessoal.name: Personnel Fiscal Agent
pessoal.role: Detects appointment spikes for political appointments during the electoral window and abnormal turnover in the same position.
pessoal.criteria_1: Five or more political appointments in the same month, within the 90 days before the municipal election — a period prohibited by TSE Resolution No. 23.570/2018.
pessoal.criteria_2: Dismissal and new appointment for the same political position within an interval of less than 90 days, with a different holder — a pattern indicative of motivated turnover.
pessoal.exclusion_1: Appointments of civil servants approved in public competitive exams (permanent civil service positions are not covered).
pessoal.exclusion_2: Dismissals at the servant's own request, with no subsequent appointment to the same position.
pessoal.exclusion_3: Appointments outside the electoral window defined by the TSE.
pessoal.legal: Federal Constitution, Art. 37, II; TSE Resolution No. 23.570/2018 (prohibition of appointments during the electoral period). Windows defined by electoral cycle (2024, 2026, 2028).

nepotismo.name: Nepotism Fiscal Agent
nepotismo.role: Detects an uncommon surname shared between a political appointee and an officeholder or department head.
nepotismo.criteria_1: An uncommon surname shared between a server appointed to a political position and the mayor, a department head, or a city councilor identified in the same act or in acts from the same period.
nepotismo.exclusion_1: High-frequency surnames nationally (Silva, Santos, Oliveira, Souza, Lima, among others) do not constitute this type.
nepotismo.exclusion_2: Career civil servants (permanent positions) are expressly excluded — Binding Precedent 13 does not apply to competitive-exam appointees.
nepotismo.exclusion_3: The Agent never asserts kinship. It reports exclusively a surname coincidence — verification is the responsibility of the competent authority and the citizen.
nepotismo.exclusion_4: This is the only Agent with a minimum confidence of 0.95 (instead of the standard 0.70). Findings below this threshold are discarded without issuing an alert — the reputational risk of an incorrect assertion justifies the elevated threshold.
nepotismo.legal: Supreme Court Binding Precedent 13 (prohibition of nepotism in Public Administration); Federal Constitution, Art. 37, caput (impersonality and morality).

publicidade.name: Advertising Fiscal Agent
publicidade.role: Detects institutional advertising contracts entered into during the period prohibited by electoral legislation.
publicidade.criteria_1: Contract, budget commitment, or commitment note for institutional advertising services, propaganda, media placements, or official communications, dated within the 90 days before the first round of the municipal election.
publicidade.criteria_2: Aggravation when the advertising material nominally mentions the mayor or the current administration — a multiplying factor for the risk score.
publicidade.exclusion_1: Notices of grave and urgent public necessity, with express authorization from the Electoral Court (Law 9.504/97, Art. 73, §12).
publicidade.exclusion_2: Advertising outside the electoral window defined by the TSE.
publicidade.exclusion_3: Legally mandatory notices (compulsory publication of official acts).
publicidade.legal: Law 9.504/97, Art. 73, Inc. VI, subparagraph "b" (prohibition of institutional advertising in the 3 months before the election). Windows calculated by election date (cycles 2024, 2026, 2028).

locacao.name: Property Lease Fiscal Agent
locacao.role: Detects property lease contracts signed on a sole-source basis without the legally required technical justifications.
locacao.criteria_1: Property lease contract on a sole-source basis (inexigibilidade) with no reference to a valuation report or market value in the published act — requirements of Art. 74, Inc. III.
locacao.criteria_2: Contracts with an estimated annual value above R$ 240,000 (equivalent to R$ 20,000/month) without the required justifications.
locacao.exclusion_1: Contracts that explicitly cite a property valuation report or a documented market value reference.
locacao.exclusion_2: Leases with a value below the relevance threshold defined by the Agent.
locacao.legal: Law 14.133/2021, Art. 74, Inc. III (sole-source for property lease with specific characteristics). The R$ 240,000/year threshold is heuristic — it will be calibrated by city when local real-estate market data is integrated.

diarias.name: Per Diem Fiscal Agent
diarias.role: Detects per diem allowances above the reference threshold and trips whose dates coincide with national holidays or weekends.
diarias.criteria_1: Per diem with a unit value above R$ 1,500.00, regardless of destination — a value above the cap practiced by the most generous state decree.
diarias.criteria_2: Travel authorization with a departure date coinciding with a national holiday (fixed and variable calendar 2024–2028) or weekend, with no documented urgency justification.
diarias.exclusion_1: Trips with urgency or service-necessity justification duly documented in the act.
diarias.exclusion_2: Per diems within the limit set by the current municipal decree for the city under analysis.
diarias.legal: Municipal per diem decrees (vary by city — the Agent uses R$ 1,500.00 as a conservative reference; it will be parameterized by city when local decrees are catalogued). National holiday calendar: fixed and variable (Easter, Carnival, Corpus Christi) 2024–2028.

convenios.name: Partnerships Fiscal Agent
convenios.role: Detects civil society organization agreements entered into without a public call for proposals and recurring transfers to the same CNPJ without a new formal instrument.
convenios.criteria_1: Cooperation or support agreement with a civil society organization (OSC/OSCIP) with no mention of a public call for proposals, a documented waiver (Art. 29), or a sole-source justification (Art. 30), when the value exceeds R$ 600,000.
convenios.criteria_2: Three or more transfers to the same CNPJ within a 12-month period without a new formal instrument — a pattern indicative of de facto continuation outside the regulatory framework.
convenios.exclusion_1: Public call for proposals conducted and cited in the act.
convenios.exclusion_2: Waiver expressly grounded in Art. 29 (urgency, calamity, education, health, or social assistance services).
convenios.exclusion_3: Sole-source justification expressly grounded in Art. 30 (uniqueness of the object or a legally accredited specific entity).
convenios.legal: Law 13.019/2014 (Civil Society Organizations Regulatory Framework), Art. 24 (public call as the general rule), Art. 29 (waiver), Art. 30 (sole-source); Arts. 33 and 35 (recurring transfers). Federal Decree 8.726/2016. Reference of R$ 600,000 observed in municipal decrees from RS, SP and MG — not a rigid statutory cap.

custos.name: FiscalCustos
custos.role: Internal transparency over the project's own costs
custos.description: Every day at 03:00 BRT, this agent queries AWS Cost Explorer with daily granularity by service, converts the values to BRL using the Brazilian Central Bank PTAX rate, and publishes the result on /transparencia/custos. Unlike the other Fiscais — which oversee municipal public spending — FiscalCustos oversees our own operational costs. The same verifiability standard we apply to public contracts we apply to ourselves.
custos.threshold_value: Current month vs. previous month > 20% (amber visual alert).

roadmap.title: Timeline
roadmap.subtitle: From the initial provocation to a pipeline in production — how Fiscal Digital was built and where it is headed.
roadmap.origin_p1: Brazil has more than 5,500 municipalities. Each one publishes an official gazette with contracts, procurement, appointments, and spending. That is millions of pages per year — public data, accessible by law, but impossible to follow at a human scale.
roadmap.origin_p2_mid: project demonstrated that algorithms can identify anomalous patterns in public spending. In 2019,
roadmap.origin_p2_end: digitized the official gazettes of hundreds of Brazilian municipalities. Two pieces of civic infrastructure built by civil society, together making something new possible.
roadmap.origin_p3: If every gazette is already digitized, what prevents an algorithm from reading the same clauses an auditor would read — and doing so for every city, every day?
roadmap.origin_p4: Fiscal Digital connects these two legacies: it uses the Querido Diário archive as its source and the Serenata principle as its method. The focus is the municipality — where everyday purchases happen, where street works are contracted, where political appointments are published.
roadmap.origin_p5: We do not investigate. We do not accuse. We identify patterns that existing legal criteria define as irregular and make the result verifiable by any citizen. The primary source — the official gazette — is always one click away.
roadmap.coverage_cities: 50 active cities across 22 states (top 50 Brazil by population + state capitals)
roadmap.coverage_origin: Origin: Caxias do Sul, RS — municipal administration 2021–present
roadmap.coverage_pipeline: Daily pipeline: 03:00 BRT, 7 days a week
roadmap.coverage_next: Next phase: gradual expansion as Querido Diário coverage grows
roadmap.costs_infra_note: Values converted from USD at R$ 5.75. AWS Bedrock prices based on public pricing as of May 2026.
roadmap.costs_funding_body: Fiscal Digital is maintained by contributions from civil society. Every inflow and outflow is public. The code is open source under the MIT license.
roadmap.eval_body: Evaluating Brazilian LLMs (data sovereignty) and expansion to 5,000 municipalities with ISR support.

timeline_items.step0_desc: Discovery of Querido Diário → the provocation
timeline_items.sprint1_desc: AWS infrastructure, Skills, Querido Diário Collector
timeline_items.sprint4_desc: End-to-end pipeline, 8,400+ gazettes processed, 12+ real alerts
timeline_items.sprint5_desc: Public RSS/JSON API, institutional site, 10 Fiscal Agents

meta.home_title: Fiscal Digital — Autonomous oversight of Brazilian municipal spending
meta.home_description: Autonomous agents read Brazilian municipal official gazettes and publish verifiable alerts. Every finding cites its source. MIT and CC-BY 4.0.
meta.alertas_description: Findings published automatically by Fiscal agents. Risk score >= 60 and confidence >= 0.70. Always with cited source.
meta.manifesto_description: Why municipal oversight is the most urgent civic work. Principles, method, limits and commitments of Fiscal Digital.
meta.sobre_description: Who is behind Fiscal Digital, the team, partners and our roots in Serenata de Amor and Querido Diário.
meta.transparencia_description: Every dollar in and every dollar out, in public. Operating costs, donations and financial reports of the project.
meta.transparencia_custos_description: Daily costs of running Fiscal Digital, refreshed every day. Collected by FiscalCustos straight from AWS Cost Explorer and converted at PTAX (Brazilian Central Bank).
meta.apoie_description: Support Fiscal Digital on Catarse. Recurring, transparent, with no governmental or partisan funding.
meta.cidades_description: Brazilian municipalities monitored by Fiscal Digital. Caxias do Sul live; expanding to 50 cities.
meta.cidade_description: Alerts, suppliers and public contracts of {cidade} extracted from the official gazette. Updated daily.
meta.alerta_description: Detail of the finding in {cidade}, with legal basis, evidence and link to the original Querido Diário gazette.
meta.comparacoes_description: Compare spending, suppliers and risk patterns across Brazilian municipalities monitored by Fiscal Digital.
meta.fiscais_description: 10 autonomous agents with explicit criteria, documented legal basis, and calibrated confidence thresholds. Auditable source code at github.com/fiscal-digital.
meta.roadmap_description: From the initial provocation to a pipeline in production. Origin story, completed sprints, real costs, and the sustainability model of Fiscal Digital.
